text: Planning a trip is always exciting. Last summer, I spent weeks researching destinations, booking flights, and choosing hotels. When I finally arrived in Japan, everything felt new. The culture fascinated me—temples, bowing greetings, and quiet trains. The food was a highlight: I tried ramen, sushi, and sweet mochi, though some dishes were surprisingly unfamiliar. Language barriers were tricky, but pointing at menus and using simple English helped. I’ll always remember the kindness of locals who helped me find my way. Those experiences—planning, discovering new cultures, tasting different foods, and overcoming communication challenges—created lasting memories.
